pi_items.metadata_value: BBBC005 (from filename pattern and task description)
pi_items.verified_value: BBBC005 - Broad Bioimage Benchmark Collection dataset (official: bbbc.broadinstitute.org/BBBC005)
pi_items.source: https://bbbc.broadinstitute.org/BBBC005 - official Broad Bioimage Benchmark Collection page
BBBC005 official documentation states two stain types: stain 1 = cell body, stain 2 = nuclei
Official BBBC005 documentation: 'stain: 1 = cell body stain, 2 = nuclei stain' at bbbc.broadinstitute.org/BBBC005
Task description explicitly states the agent should create a Hyperstack with '1 Timeframe'

pi_items.verified_value: Pattern matches BBBC005 naming scheme: A01 (well), C1 (cell count), .* (focal plane), s1 (sample starting with 1)
pi_items.source: BBBC005 filename format is SIMCEPImages_well_Ccells_Fblur_ssamples_wstain.TIF. Example: A01_C1_F1_s01_w1.TIF
Install script (install_fiji.sh) downloads BBBC005_v1_images.zip from official Broad Institute repository
Official BBBC005 documentation and multiple academic sources confirm 19,200 total image files in BBBC005 v1
Official BBBC005 documentation specifies image format
BBBC005 consists of simulated/synthetic fluorescence microscopy images generated with SIMCEP platform, not real biological samples

pi_items.metadata_value: Multiple (F1, F2, etc.) implied by task
pi_items.verified_value: Images use blur parameter (F1-F48) simulating different focus conditions, with F1 being fully in-focus
pi_items.source: Official BBBC005 documentation: blur ranges 1-48 to simulate defocus, not distinct focal planes

privileged_info_summary: BBBC005 is a real, publicly available synthetic dataset from the Broad Bioimage Benchmark Collection containing 19,200 simulated fluorescence microscopy images (696x520 pixels, 8-bit TIF). Data is available at https://data.broadinstitute.org/bbbc/BBBC005/. The dataset uses two stains: stain 1 = cell body, stain 2 = nuclei. CRITICAL CONTRADICTION: The task description states 'w1=Nuclear, w2=Cell Body' but official documentation shows w1 corresponds to cell body stain and w2 corresponds to nuclei stain—the channel assignments are reversed in the task description. Task requires reconstructing a 4D hyperstack (2 channels, 1 timeframe, multiple Z slices) for well A01, cell density C1, site s1.
